notes: All 15 responses get the chart skeleton right (title 'Ocean acidification', Year 1958-2018, CO2 275-425 left axis, pH 8.03-8.33 right axis, three series with correct station attribution: red Atmospheric CO2 at Mauna Loa, green Ocean pCO2 at ALOHA, blue Ocean pH at ALOHA). Atmospheric CO2 values (315->415 ppm) are uniformly accurate. Two systematic, model-wide errors recur: (1) ocean pCO2 starting value is inflated from the actual ~325 ppm to ~350 ppm in nearly every response, and (2) ocean pH starting value is inflated from ~8.12 to ~8.18-8.25. The single worst error is t0.4|rep1 fabricating 1958 start dates for the ocean series, which visibly begin ~1988-1990. The best numeric accuracy is t0.7|rep1, whose pCO2 endpoint (~380) and pH endpoint (~8.06) both match the chart. Temperature had no clear monotonic effect on quality; the spread is driven by individual value accuracy, not sampling temperature.
